headline: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) Expands Cancer Pipeline As Talc Verdict Eases One Risk
summary: Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) received positive regulatory developments in Europe for multiple myeloma therapy Tecvayli used in combination with Darzalex SC, supported by phase III trial data. The company agreed to acquire Firefly Bio to expand its cancer drug pipeline into next generation oncology treatments. A Los Angeles jury delivered a verdict in favor of Johnson & Johnson in a key talc litigation case related to ovarian cancer. Johnson & Johnson sits at the intersection of...

headline: The Portfolio That Pays For Your Daughter’s Wedding
summary: Most parents approach a wedding as a bill. Save the money, write the checks, and move on. Investors can look at it differently. Instead of asking how much the wedding will cost, ask how much capital would be required to generate that amount from portfolio income. The answer reveals something interesting: the wedding lasts one ... The Portfolio That Pays For Your Daughter’s Wedding

headline: Medicare Keeps You Alive. This Portfolio Keeps You Looking Good.
summary: Hearing aids restore conversations. Dental work restores smiles. Vision correction restores independence. Many of the expenses Medicare leaves uncovered sit at the intersection of appearance, confidence, and daily quality of life. Unfortunately, all those things cost money. Big money. So here’s how to close the gap in your budget… and your teeth. What Medicare Leaves ... Medicare Keeps You Alive. This Portfolio Keeps You Looking Good.

headline: Here’s How Much Money You Need to Replace a $50,000 Income With Dividends
summary: The median U.S. household income is roughly $50,000 a year. It’s also a common floor for a livable retirement budget once Social Security benefits are layered on top. Replacing it with dividends alone is a math problem before it is a stock-picking problem, and the inputs are blunt: The yield you accept determines the capital ... Here’s How Much Money You Need to Replace a $50,000 Income With Dividends
